methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $141M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $600M - $3.3B.

methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 7.8x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
AAVantgarde Bio is a Series B-stage biotech company founded by Alberto Auricchio and Natalia Misciattelli that has raised $141M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 8 private and 3 public companies in Biotech & Health, anchored to five same-stage peers: Eikon Therapeutics ($3.3B), Neko Health ($1.8B), New Limit ($810M), Asimov ($800M), and Profluent ($600M).

Two methods triangulate AAVantgarde's value. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x multiplier to the $141M total raised proxy, yielding $268M. Peer-median analysis across the five same-sector, same-stage comparables produces $810M, with a range of $600M to $3.3B. Public-comp anchoring is inapplicable due to absence of revenue disclosure, typical for private companies. Public Biotech & Health peers trade at a median 7.8x price-to-sales across three tickers (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L), but cannot be applied without target revenue data.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $228M to $931M, with a median of $539M. The wide dispersion between stage-progression and peer-median results reflects heterogeneity in peer maturity and capital efficiency. Key uncertainties include the target's clinical-stage positioning relative to peers and whether the peer set adequately reflects AAVantgarde's asset profile. Confidence in the median improves if the company's development stage aligns with the lower-quartile peer group ($600M–$810M).
